name: Cistercian Monastery of Santa Maria from Granja de Moruela
name_original: Monasterio Cisterciense de Santa Maria desde Granja de Moruela
region_intro: Hiking around Granja De Moreruela offers routes through varied landscapes, including wetlands, riverbanks, and historical sites. The region is characterized by the presence of the Río Esla and nearby lagoons, providing opportunities for bird watching and tranquil walks. Trails often feature flat terrain, with some routes passing by the Cistercian Monastery of Santa María. The area provides a mix of natural and cultural exploration. Best hiking trails around Granja De Moreruela The most popular hiking route is Laguna Salina Grande — return via the Villafáfila Lagoons Natural Reserve, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete, offering views of the saline lagoon. Another top favourite among local hikers is Río Esla from Puente Quintos – circular by Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés, an easy 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This route follows the riverbanks and incorporates a section of the historic Camino Mozárabe. Local hikers also love the Cistercian Monastery of Santa Maria from Granja de Moruela, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through rural landscapes to the historic monastery, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Hiking around Granja De Moreruela is defined by river paths, lagoon views, and historical sites like the Moreruela Monastery. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Granja De Moreruela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Granja De Moreruela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Granja De Moreruela?
A: There are 9 hiking trails around Granja De Moreruela. These include a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various experience levels.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Granja De Moreruela?
A: Yes, Granja De Moreruela offers 4 easy hiking routes suitable for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123258047/rio-esla-from-puente-quintos-circular-by-camino-mozarabe-sanabres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Esla from Puente Quintos – circular by Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.6 km long and follows scenic riverbanks.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Granja De Moreruela?
A: The region features diverse landscapes, including tranquil wetlands, the banks of the Río Esla, and open rural areas. You'll encounter lagoons, offering opportunities for bird watching, and historical sites like the Cistercian Monastery of Santa María.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Granja De Moreruela?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306735956/camino-sanabres-and-moreruela-monastery-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Sanabrés and Moreruela Monastery Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an 8 km moderate trail that brings you back to your starting point after exploring the monastery area.

Q: What historical sites can I visit on a hike?
A: Many trails incorporate historical landmarks. You can hike to the Cistercian Monastery of Santa María, a significant historical site. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025267178/cistercian-monastery-of-santa-maria-from-granja-de-moruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cistercian Monastery of Santa Maria from Granja de Moruela&lt;/a&gt; route is an easy 7.8 km path leading directly to it.

Q: Are there opportunities for bird watching along the trails?
A: Absolutely. The region's wetlands and lagoons, particularly around the Villafáfila Lagoons Natural Reserve, are excellent for bird watching. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Otero de Sariegos Bird Observatory&lt;/a&gt; or explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salina Grande Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Granja De Moreruela?
A: The trails in Granja De Moreruela are generally well-regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.0 stars. Hikers often appreciate the tranquil river paths, the scenic lagoon views, and the historical significance of sites like the Moreruela Monastery.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions to see?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, you can explore natural highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salina Grande Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and the broader &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5508361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafáfila Lagoons Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, which are important wetland areas.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Camino Mozárabe?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate sections of the historic Camino Mozárabe. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123258047/rio-esla-from-puente-quintos-circular-by-camino-mozarabe-sanabres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Esla from Puente Quintos – circular by Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that follows both the riverbanks and a part of this ancient pilgrimage path.

Q: What is the longest and most challenging hike in the area?
A: The most challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123176342/camino-sanabres-moreruela-monastery-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Sanabrés: Moreruela Monastery Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult trail spans approximately 21.6 km and typically takes over 5 hours to complete, offering a more extensive exploration of the region.

Q: What are some interesting man-made landmarks or structures to look out for?
A: Beyond the monastery, you can find several bridges over the Esla River, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/769731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintos Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/926254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. There's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Church and Pilgrim Hostel&lt;/a&gt; in Granja de Moreruela itself.
tips:
The population of Granja de Moreruel was born by the impulse of the Monastery of Santa María, raised by the eagerness of the Leonese monarchs to colonize extensive wastelands.
Granja de Moreruela also has a church built in the mid-19th century, the Church of San Juan.
Its term is crossed by the old Vía de la Plata, the Camino de Santiago de la Vía de la Plata and its variant, the Camino Mozárabe or Sanabrés. There is a Pilgrims Hostel.

At this point we come across the extensive meadow of La Guadaña where we can listen to and see a great variety of birds while we walk the path towards (probably) the Cistercian Monastery of Santa Maria de Moreruela.

One of the most beautiful and magnificent Cistercian monasteries in the province of Zamora is the Cistercian Monastery of Santa Maria, in the municipality of Granja de Moreruela.
The monastery preserves the size of the imposing Cistercian abbey ordered to be built by Alfonso VII. Built at the end of the 12th century, it is in the Romanesque style with elements of transition to Gothic.
The chancel is the largest part of the complex, formed by a superposition of levels: apses, a polygonal ambulatory and the central apse.
The walls of the Monastery are used by white storks to breed
